title: Ora Canvas State v0.1
description: Editable canvas workspace per Visual Intelligence Implementation Plan §11.7. Represents the durable state of a VisualPanel: layer list, object list (with embedded base64 image data), saved view (zoom + pan offset), and metadata. The same payload round-trips through both surfacing forms — `.ora-canvas` (gzip-compressed default) and `.ora-canvas.json` (uncompressed export-on-demand). additionalProperties:false at every level by Ora schema convention (envelope.json §2.1).

schema_version.description: Semver. Readers fail-closed on unknown major versions.

format_id.description: Magic identifier. Distinguishes a canvas-state document from any other JSON Ora may emit.

title.description: Optional user-facing title for the canvas.

conversation_id.description: Optional link back to the orchestrator conversation that produced or owns this canvas.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of initial creation. Set by the writer; not modified by subsequent saves.

modified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent save. The writer SHOULD set this on every write.

canvas_size.description: Nominal pixel dimensions of the workspace per §11.7 (default 10000 x 10000). Distinct from the saved bounding-box extent.

content_extent.description: Optional bounding box of all content at save time, used by the §11.7 'bounded-to-content-extent' export semantics. Coordinates are in canvas pixel space.

ora_version.description: Free-form Ora build identifier. Diagnostics only — readers MUST NOT branch on this.

view.description: Saved view restored when the canvas is reopened (§11.7 'returning to a saved session').

layers.description: Ordered list of layers, bottom -> top. Object membership is by `layer` field on the object, referencing layer.id.

id.description: Unique within the layers array. Object.layer references this id.

kind.description: Mirrors the four-layer Konva model in visual-panel.js (background / annotation / user_input / selection).

objects.description: Flat list of all canvas objects across all layers. Order within a layer is render order (bottom -> top).

object.description: Discriminated by `kind`. shape/text/group/path inline a Konva-style attrs payload; image inlines base64 data per §11.7 'no external references'.

id.description: Unique within the canvas. Used by selection state and reference fields on other objects.

kind.description: shape  = Konva primitive (rect/ellipse/line/arrow/diamond/etc.); text = standalone text node; image = raster with embedded base64 data; group = container with children[]; path = freehand pen stroke.

z_index.description: Optional stacking-order override within the host layer. When absent, array order applies.

konva_class.description: Optional Konva className (e.g. 'Rect', 'Ellipse', 'Line', 'Arrow', 'Text'). Lets the loader pick the correct constructor when round-tripping shape/text/path objects.

attrs.description: Konva-shaped attribute bag (see Konva.Node.toJSON()). Free-form by intent — Konva owns its own attr surface and Ora must not constrain it. The discriminator is `kind` + `konva_class`; `attrs` is the payload.

children.description: For kind=group: nested objects in render order.

image_data.description: Inline image payload. REQUIRED for kind=image (no external references per §11.7).

mime_type.description: MIME type of the image bytes (e.g. image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, image/svg+xml).

encoding.description: Encoding of `data`. Only base64 is defined in v0.1 per §11.2 'Image embedding within canvas file: Base64-in-JSON, inline'.

data.description: Base64-encoded image bytes. Raw payload only -- MUST NOT include a 'data:<mime>;base64,' prefix; readers reconstruct the data URL from mime_type + data when needed.

natural_width.description: Optional intrinsic pixel width of the source image.

natural_height.description: Optional intrinsic pixel height of the source image.

source.description: Optional provenance hint -- 'upload', 'paste', 'generated:<provider>', etc. Diagnostics only.

user_label.description: Optional human-authored label, mirrors visual-panel's userLabel attr.

metadata.description: Free-form per-object metadata bag. Reserved for capability-specific annotations (generation prompt, edit history, etc.).
